name: people
description: The CENTRAL-plane person registry — cross-academy shared profile substrate per blueprint 2.2. Owns the human-readable Stackra ID (AX-XXXX-XXXX) and issues one PersonIdentity row per real human GLOBAL across tenants + Applications. Ships THREE owned entities: `PersonIdentity` (central; minimal PII — legal_name, dob, nationality, verified_at; NO tenant_id), `PersonGuardianLink` (central; guardian↔minor link that survives cross-tenant), `TenantLinkRequest` (consent-gated request from a tenant to link a local Athlete/Staff to a global PersonIdentity — state requested → approved / declined → revoked; enumeration-resistant lookup requires ID + DOB and reveals no PII pre-consent). Tenant `athletes` / `staff` rows carry a NULLABLE `person_identity_id` FK back into this central registry — this is the ONLY cross-DB reference in the platform, and it points from tenant DBs INTO central (never the reverse). Consent flow: guardian receives notification of link request → picks a shareable subset (e.g. emergency contact, allergies, passport doc) → tenant B receives a local Athlete pre-filled with only consented fields → adds its own sport enrollment + cards which stay 100% tenant-local. Revoking a link stops future sync, but each academy keeps the local data it created (subject to retention/GDPR). Rate-limited lookup (10/hr per IP + 100/day per originating tenant) + monotonic 3-of-5 sliding-window failed-attempts lockout.

consent_architecture.description: Consent to link a local tenant record to a global PersonIdentity is per-tenant and REVOCABLE. Revocation stops future sync but preserves the local data the receiving tenant already created (subject to its own retention/GDPR policies).
shareable_field_subset: passport_document_id (points at storage::File on origin tenant with cross-tenant signed URL)
never_shared: scoring / cards / attendance / progress (100% tenant-local per blueprint 2.2)
consent_architecture.lookup_rate_limit: 10 lookups / hour / IP; 100 / day / originating tenant; 3-of-5 sliding-window failed attempts trigger 24h lockout

tenant.description: Tenant-facing surface: search for existing PersonIdentity (ID + DOB), file a link request, view + revoke existing links for the tenant.

platform-admin.description: Platform-plane CRUD on the PersonIdentity registry — issue verification, resolve disputes, freeze fraudulent identities.

central.description: Public consent-approval surface — guardian receives a link like /people/link-requests/{signature}, reviews, picks shareable fields, approves or declines.
